world_kind.tone_anchor: Daniel Tiger with animal tendencies — neighbors talk, reason, barter, hold grudges, but retain instincts (alertness, food drives, territory). Non-violent.
world_kind.violence_rule: Physical contact is emotional display, not harm. No one gets hurt. Conflict resolves through words, trade, or retreat.
world_kind.speech_rule: All named animals can speak and reason. Instinct colors voice — a hungry squirrel speaks tersely; a startled bird speaks too fast — but does not block speech.

geography.region_anchor: Northeast US — temperate deciduous, mixed hardwood forest, four-season, fieldstone walls, ferny understory.

home_base.label: the counting tree
home_base.description: An old red oak with a hollow YY sleeps in. Carries forward from root_2026_04_14 as the persistent home node. Bark notched on the south side from a winter that left a mark.
home_base.source: carried forward from runs/root_2026_04_14 (geography.home_base derivative — same role, sharper definition under v2.0)

named_places.label: the brook crossing
named_places.description: A shallow rocky brook running west-east, fordable except in spring runoff. Cold, clear, lined with skunk cabbage in early spring.
named_places.notes: Common watering point — neighbors meet here without it being a meeting.

named_places.label: the fallen oak
named_places.description: A downed hardwood half-buried in leaf litter, with several cache hollows along its length. Communal-ish — caches here are tolerated, not defended.
named_places.notes: First-come-first-served. Reputation accrues to those who refill.

named_places.label: the south meadow
named_places.description: Open grassland sloping south. Wild blueberries in summer. Exposed sightlines — hawks visible from a long way off.
named_places.notes: High-risk, high-reward. Mira forages here in late summer.

named_places.label: the stone wall
named_places.description: Old fieldstone wall along the northern range. Half-tumbled, moss-furred. Used as a path more than a barrier.
named_places.notes: Tock perches on the higher stones to gossip.

named_places.label: the resin pines
named_places.description: A small stand of white pines on the western slope. Resin pools form on bark wounds — gather-able but takes patience.
named_places.notes: Source of resin_strip items. Worth the trip on slow days.

geography.territory_notes: YY's range centers on the counting tree, with the brook crossing and fallen oak as the most-traveled neighbors. The stone wall is the northern soft-edge; the south meadow is the southern reach. The resin pines are westward and only worth the trip when the day is slow.

society.capability: Neighbors speak, reason, hold relationships and slow grudges. Trades are remembered. Animal instincts persist (a startled rabbit still bolts) but do not block social behavior.
customs: Fair trades are expected. Uneven trades are remembered.
Greeting at first crossing of the day is polite — silence is not rude but it is noticed.
Helping a neighbor through a hard week creates a quiet debt; it is repaid without being named.

neighbors.name: Bramble
neighbors.notes: Recently emerged from torpor — hungry, friendly, overpromises in trades. Means well. Will give YY food if YY listens patiently to a story first.

neighbors.name: Mira
neighbors.notes: Sharp on trades. Reads weather before others do. Holds slow grudges — a bad trade is remembered for weeks. Recognizes status items immediately.

neighbors.name: Tock
neighbors.notes: Knows where everyone is and who saw what. Gossip is currency. Will trade information for food, but the information's accuracy varies with the trade's generosity.

currencies.use: Common, perishable, abundance varies seasonally. Used for everyday trades and small favors.
Rarer, durable, store-of-value. Trades for these are slower and more deliberate.

economy.exchange_norms: Barter-based. Fair trades are the norm; uneven trades are remembered. Prices drift with season and scarcity. Tock will accept gossip as currency in either direction.
economy.advancement: Foraging and exploring improve standing. Finding rare materials or productive locations creates wealth. Hoarding without trade is socially flat — wealth that doesn't move doesn't grow.

mobility.method: Physical exertion only. Paws, claws, tails. No vehicles, no machinery.
mobility.constraints: Travel cost is real — long trips spend food and attention. The brook crossing is a half-day from home; the south meadow is most of a day. The resin pines are a full day's commitment.

seasonal_arc.compression: 30 run-days ≈ Jan → Dec, weighted toward shoulder seasons. Winter framing at both ends; spring/summer/autumn carry the narrative weight.
seasonal_arc.weather_register: Northeast US temperate — mud season, late frosts, leaf-out, summer thunderstorms, fall foliage, first snow.

schedule.weather: Hard ground, last patches of snow in shaded hollows, sharp wind. Sun rises but does not warm.
Mud season — softening ground, late frosts at night, first buds, skunk cabbage at the brook. Days lengthen visibly.
Warming, leaf-out completes, occasional thunderstorms in the afternoon. Air thickens.
Humid, hot afternoons, thunderstorms most days, blueberries in the south meadow.
First cool nights, foliage starting in the maples, hard light, dry air. The harvest window.
Hard frosts, leaves down, cold winds, the south meadow goes brown. Cache-building urgency.
First real snow, hardening ground, breath visible. The world quiets.

inventory_catalog._note: Initial vocabulary of unique items. Additional items may emerge through events.

items.label: the heron feather
items.description: A long blue-grey flight feather, found near water. Recognizable as exotic — herons pass through but rarely shed where they can be seen.
interaction_unlocks: Tock recognizes it on sight and will trade extra gossip to hold it briefly.
Mira evaluates it as status, not utility — affects how she reads YY's social standing.
Bramble admires it loudly and asks to see it whenever they cross.

items.label: the green-streaked stone
items.description: A river-smoothed pebble with a vein of green. Pretty in the right light.
interaction_unlocks: Mira will trade gathered_materials for it if her mood is fair.
Useful as a small gift to soften a bad trade.

items.label: a resin-sealed bark strip
items.description: Bark with a hardened pool of pine resin still attached. Glue, currency, or fire-starter.
interaction_unlocks: Trades evenly with Mira for hazelnuts in late winter.
Bramble wants these and overpays in food.

items.label: a perfect pinecone
items.description: Unusually intact, scales open but seeds still inside. Trade-grade.
interaction_unlocks: High trade value with any neighbor.

items.label: a stoneware shard
items.description: An edge of broken pottery, glazed blue on one side. Human-made — uncertain value, definite curiosity.
interaction_unlocks: Tock has theories and will share them in exchange for one.
Mira distrusts human-made things and will not trade for it.

items.label: a winter-survived hazelnut
items.description: A hazelnut from last fall that survived the winter intact. Edible. Slightly stale but precious in late winter.
interaction_unlocks: Bramble lights up at the sight of one and trades generously.
Counts as a gift in the late-winter customary register.

starting_conditions._note: Late winter — Day 1 lands at days 1-3 of the seasonal_arc. Food is honestly low, attention is high (emergence-alert), health is winter-thinned but intact.
identity_notes: Late winter waking — YY emerges from the counting tree hungry and alert.
First root in the new world (v2.0). Neighbors and economy are felt for the first time. No accumulated debts or grudges yet.

atmosphere.one_line: A whole year compressed into a month — emergence, abundance, harvest, shutdown — through the eyes of one squirrel and three neighbors.
recurring_elements: the brook running through every season
Tock's gossip as a low constant
the stone wall as the persistent navigational marker
the slow change in food availability shaping every trade
weather a NE-US reader can name without being told
atmosphere.what_this_month_costs: Late-winter scarcity at the open and early-winter scarcity at the close. Travel cost is real. Trades that go bad with Mira compound for weeks.
atmosphere.what_this_month_offers: An entire seasonal arc in one root. The chance to watch YY learn the social field from scratch. Every neighbor has different incentives; every season changes which trades make sense.

authorial_intent.arc_direction: Track YY through one compressed year in a populated, anthropomorphic, NE-anchored world. Watch how social standing accumulates, how the seasonal arc reshapes trade, and how a single inherited status item changes 30 days of interactions.
questions_to_track: Does Mira's first impression of YY become a stable read or a revisable one?
What does Bramble's enthusiasm cost YY when it goes wrong?
Does Tock's gossip economy reward generosity or stinginess?
How does the food_pressure curve interact with trade decisions — does YY save or trade in surplus?
authorial_intent.branch_hypothesis: alt1 starts Day 1 holding the heron_feather — a single exotic status item from some unseen prior moment. Main starts with nothing. Hypothesis: a single inherited status object reshapes 30 days of social positioning. Mira reads alt1 as higher status from Day 1; Bramble approaches with more deference and bigger asks; Tock circles harder, trying to broker its temporary loan. Main has to earn each interaction from a flat start. The divergence is small at root start (one item) and we want to see whether it converges, compounds, or surprises us by being orthogonal to wealth.
